methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Cache Energy, a Seed-stage company in Climate & Energy founded by Arpit Dwivedi, has raised $8.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), all operating in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $8.5M raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage, implies a valuation of $21M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Watershed ($1.8B Series A), Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—yielding a median valuation of $175M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B. The wide range reflects heterogeneity within the cohort.

Triangulating across both methods produces a valuation range of $18M (low) to $201M (high), with a median implied value of $98M. This wide spread reflects the tension between the stage-progression method—which yields a conservative estimate based on capital deployed—and peer multiples, which carry material uncertainty given the nascency of the sector, disparity in peer valuations, and limited visibility into Cache Energy's specific competitive position and technology maturity. Key uncertainties include comparability of business models across the peer set and the sustainability of current climate-tech valuations in the private market.
